ety: nibal, q.v.+Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Powerlessness, impotence, weakness, prostration of strength (i.q. nirbalāʼī).

s.m. Binding, fastening, tying; attachment; composing; — restraint, confinement, obstruction; constipation; suppression of urine, strangury; — a band, bond, fetter; fixed property, a grant (of property); — a literary composition, a treatise.
adj. Fixed, determined, settled; affixed; ascertained (syn. ṭhahrāyā).

s.m. A binding, fastening, tie, ligation; building, construction; checking, restraining; restraint, confinement; — band, fetter; — ratification; — a composition; (in Gram.) syntax; — firmness; — that to which anything is fastened, or on which it rests; a receptacle; key, or peg (of a lute, &c.); — cause, origin; reason, motive; condition.
s.f. Band, bond, fetter.
adj. (f. -inī), Binding; confining; — joined, or connected (by, or with), bound (to); hanging together; — causing, being a cause (of).
part. (f. -ā), Bound, fastened, tied; confined.
s.m. (dialec.) Settlement, adjustment, decision; — passing, passage, transit; getting through, deliverance.
s.m. = نوارن niwāraṇ, q.v.
s.m. Separation; deciding, decision, adjustment. settlement, disposal; accomplishing, accomplishment; ending, cessation, &c.
v.t. = نوارنا niwārnā, q.v.

ety: nibāṛ˚ = Prk. निव्वाड(इ) or निव्वाडे(इ) = S. निर्वाटय(ति), caus. of rt. निर्+वट्
def: v.t. To separate, divide; to adjust, settle, decide; to accomplish, perform, manage, fulfil, carry through, keep (an engagement, &c.); to end; to spend, to consume.

ety: nibaṛ˚ = Prk. निव्वड(इ) or निव्वडे(इ); S. निर्वटय(ति), rt. निर् + वट्; — or nibaṛnā = nibaṭnā = nipaṭnā, q.v.
def: v.n. To be separated, or divided; to be settled, be adjusted, be decided; to be accomplished; to be completed, or finished; to be ended, be spent, be consumed
v.n. = nipaṭnā, and nibaṛnā, qq.v.
s.m. Stating, representing, or relating (to a superior), representation (syn. ʻarẓ), an address, a petition (i.q. nivedan)
subs: To make representation, to represent, to state (respectfully); to make application, to plead.

def: s.m. corr. of nibedan, or nivedan, qq.v.

ety: fr. nibehnā, a dialec. form of nibāhnā, q.v.
def: adj. (dialec.) Uninterrupted, continuous, continual, unceasing, constant, lasting, permanent (i.q. nibāhū, q.v.).
s.m. Accomplishment, finishing; finish, end; settlement, adjustment, decision (i.q. nibṭerā).
s.f. (dialec.) Accomplishment; finishing; settlement, &c. (i.q. nibeṛā; nibṭerā); — a young woman (syn. yuvatī).

ety: nibeṛ˚ = Prk. निव्वंड(इ) = S. निर्वण्ड(ते), rt. निर्+वण्ड्; cf. nibāṛnā, of which nibeṛnā is another form
def: v.t. To separate, divide; to accomplish; to settle, adjust, decide; to complete, finish, terminate, put an end to; to spend, consume
adj. & s.m. Accomplishing, performing, finishing, &c.; — accomplisher, performer, finisher; adjuster, decider, &c.
adj. (f. -ā), Like, resembling, similar (only occurring as last member of compounds; sometimes pleonastically after adj.).
adj. (f. -ī), Unlucky, unfortunate; poor (also nirbhāgī).
v.t. = نباهنا nibāhnā, q.v.
s.m. Accomplishing, accomplishment, &c. (i.q. nibāh, q.v.); — continuance; permanence; durability.
v.n. To be accomplished, or performed, or effected; to succeed; — to serve, do, pass; to live, subsist, eke out a livelihood; to last, continue, endure.
adj. (dialec.) Without dignity, or respect; disgraced, dishonoured; worthless.
adj. = niviḍ, q.v.
v.n. (colloq.) = nibhnā, q.v.
s.f. The fruit or berry of the nīm tree (also niṅbaulī; rustic nibaurī).
v.t. To settle, adjust, decide, &c. (see nipṭānā)
s.m. Determination, settlement, &c. (i.q. nipṭāʼo, and nibṭerā, qq.v.).
s.m. Settlement, adjustment, determination, decision, discharge; accomplishment, completion.
s.m. The lime, Citrus medica (tree and fruit; — also nimbū, nīṅbū, nīmū, līmū, lembū)
subs: s.m. One who expresses lime-juice; — (met.) a self-invited guest; a loafer.

ety: nibuk˚ = S. निर्मुक्त, p.p.p. of निर्+मुच्
def: v.t. (dialec.) To loosen, to set free, liberate; to separate; to sunder, disjoin (syn. ćhuṛānā); — v.n. To come out (of); to be loosed, or set free (from), to escape, &c. (i.q. nikalnā).

ety: Prk. नीचो, or नीचं; S. नीचः, or नीचं
def: adj. Low; situated in a low position; deep (down); — short, small, dwarfish; — low, vile, base, mean; petty, insignificant, inferior
subs: s.f. The high and the low; — inequalities; unevenness; — the ups and downs (of life)
or nīć-zāt, s.f. Low caste; the lower orders; — a man of low caste
s.m. (f. -in, or nī), A goldsmith or jeweller of low caste
s.f. Low or vile earnings, ill-gotten gain
vulg. nīćag, adj. (f. -ā), Going downwards, descending (as a river); — being in, or belonging to, a low man, or a low class of men; low, vile, base
s.m. (in Astrol.) The house or station of the heavens in which a planet stands at its lowest point
s.m. (dialec.) A low-caste barber; an inferior sort of barber.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Low (in situation or position); depressed, &c. (i.q. nīć, q.v.)
subs: adj. (f. nīćī-ūṅćī), Up and down, high and low, uneven, not level or equal; abrupt
(-kā), To look high and low, or at the top and bottom (of); to look at (a thing) all round; to scan minutely; to ponder well
v.n. To fall low; to come below; — to be subdued or humbled
v.n. 'To look low'; to be disgraced, or dishonoured
(obl. of nīćā; Prk. nīće; S. nīće), postp. & adv. Below, under, beneath, at the bottom (of, -ke); down, below
v.n. To come down, to descend, to dismount, &c.; to fall down; — to come under
adv. Up and down; — one above and the other below; — upside-down, topsy-turvy
v.n. To go down, to descend; to subside; to submit, to succumb
To sing with a low voice, to hum (a tune); — jocose autem crepitus ventris hacce voce subintelligitur
From bottom to top; from head to foot
v.t. To lower; to hang down (the head); to degrade; to abase
v.t. To throw or hurl down; to knock down
To bend the neck, or hang down the head (from shame or humility)

ety: nīćā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Lowness; depth; declivity, &c. (i.q. nićān, q.v.); humbleness, inferiority, &c. ( = nīć-pan, q.v.).
s.f. = nićāʼī, the com. form, q.v.

ety: nīća or nīćā, q.v.+lā = Prk. ल्लओ, or लओ = S. ल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Lower, nether, under; — s.m. The lower one, the one underneath.

ety: Prk. निच्चलअं; S. निश्चल+कं, rt. चल् with निस्
def: adj. & adv. Motionless, still, quiet (e.g. nićlā baiṭh daṅgā mat kar).

ety: nīćā, q.v.+ān = Ap. Prk. अणु = Prk. अणं (for त्तणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: adj. (f. -kī), Low, depressed (ground, &c.); situated in a hollow, or in a valley, &c.
s.m. & f. = nićān, the com. form, q.v.
v.t. = nićuṛwānā, q.v.
v.n. (dialec.) = nićaṛnā, q.v.

ety: nićaṛ˚ = Prk. णिञ्चल(इ) = S. निश्चर(ति), rt. निस्+चर्
def: v.n. To flow out gently, to trickle, ooze, drop, distil; — to be squeezed or pressed; to be wrung (see nićoṛnā).
v.t. To cause to flow, or trickle, &c.; — to have or get (water, &c.) wrung out, or strained, &c.; — to cause to wring, or press, or strain, &c.

ety: nīćā, q.v.+ās = wās = wāns = wāṅćh = Prk. वंछा = S. वाञ्छा
def: s.f. Lowness, &c. (i.q. nićān, and nićāʼī, qq.v.); lowliness, humbleness; inferiority; baseness, &c. (i.q. nīć-pan, q.v.).
(prob. akin to nićaṛnā, q.v.), s.f.? (dialec.) = viśesh, q.v.
s.f. = S نيچتو नीचत्व nīćatva, s.m. = nīćpan, q.v.
s.m. = S نيچتا नीचता nīćatā, s.f. = nīćpan, q.v.
s.m. = niśćay, q.v. (cf. nihćai).
s.m. Piling or heaping up; — a collection, heap, multitude, quantity, store, stock; an assemblage.
s.m. = niśćay, q.v. (cf. nihćai).
s.m. Name of one of the degraded castes sprung from the outcast or Vrātya Kshatriyas.
adv. See s.v. nīćā.
s.f. corr. of nićintāʼī, q.v.
adj. = niśćeshṭ, q.v.
adj. Unadulterated; untainted; pure, genuine; free from flaw or defect, &c.

ety: ni = ne = new = nem = Prk. नेअम = S. नियम+ćhā = Prk. छय = S. क्षय+war = S. वरः
def: s.m. A propitiatory offering, a sacrifice; a victim; — money which is scattered at marriages and on other festive occasions (i.q. nis̤ār, q.v.); a present or offering (to a great personage); — the price paid for an idol
adj. Free from thought, or anxiety, or care; thoughtless, void of reflection, inconsiderate, heedless, unconcerned, careless; — at leisure
subs: or nićint honā or ho-jānā (-se), To be free from care or anxiety (in respect of), to be at leisure, be disengaged; to be unconcerned (about), &c.

ety: nićint, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Freedom from care or anxiety, &c.; leisure; thoughtlessness, heedlessness, unconcern, fearlessness.
adj. (colloq. or rustic) = nićīt = nićint, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) = nićint, q.v.
adj. = nićlā, the prop. form, q.v.
s.m. A cover, wrapper, mantle, outer garment, surtout; a sort of jacket or vest; a bodice.
s.m. A cover, wrapper, mantle, outer garment, surtout; a sort of jacket or vest; a bodice.
s.f. dim. of and = nićol, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = نچوڙ nićoṛ, q.v.
s.m. That which is wrung or pressed out; expressed juice (of); extract; outcome; essence, substance, gist, pith; — end, termination (of an affair).
v.t. prop. نچڙانا nićuṛānā = نچڙوانا nićaṛwānā, q.v.
s.m. = nićoṛwā, q.v.
v.t. (dialec.) = nićoṛnā, q.v.

ety: caus. of nićuṛnā, a dialec. form of nićaṛnā; — nićoṛ˚ = nićāṛ˚ = Prk. निच्चाले(इ) = S. निश्चारय(ति), caus. of rt. निस्+चर्
def: v.t. 'To cause to issue or come forth'; to press or squeeze, to wring; to press out, to express; to strain; to wring out, to extort, exact; — to milk
adj. & s.m. Exacting, extortionate, rapacious; niggardly; — one who wrings (anything) out of, an extortioner; a plunderer; — a niggard, miser.

ety: nićoṛ˚+wā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः; — or = nićoṛū+S. कः
def: adj. & s.m. Exacting, extortionate, rapacious; niggardly; — one who wrings (anything) out of, an extortioner; a plunderer; — a niggard, miser.
v.t. prop. نچڙوانا nićurwānā, q.v.
s.m. Lowness, humbleness, inferiority; vileness, baseness, meanness, abjectness.
adj. A dialec. form of nīćū, q.v.
adv. (dialec.) = nīće, q.v.
s.m. The tree Barringtonia acutangula.
s.f. Sleep (i.q. nīṅd, q.v.).
s.f. Calling; call (to prayer, &c.); proclamation, edict; — bid (at an auction); — sound, voice, clamour; a call, or voice (from heaven); — (in Gram.) the vocative case
subs: 'The particle of calling,' the vocative particle; an interjection.

def: s.m. Nest (of a bird); — lair, or den (of a beast); — the interior or seat of a carriage or chariot
subs: vulg. nīḍaj, s.m. (f. -jā), or nīḍod-bhav (˚ḍa+ud˚), s.m. 'Nest-born'; a bird.

def: s.m. Heat, warmth; the hot season (May and June); — internal heat; sweat, perspiration
s.f. Weeding, clearing; — reaping.

ety: for A. ندائية nidāʼīyat, fem. of nidāʼī, rel. adj. fr. nidā, q.v.
def: adj. Exclamatory, ejaculatory; annunciatory
subs: s.m. An exclamatory sentence or phrase, &c.

ety: prob. for nīṛal = S. नीर+लः = Prk. डो
def: s.m. (dialec.) Water.

ety: S. निदानं, rt. dā with ni
def: adv. In the end, at last, at length, eventually, finally, after all; altogether; in brief; at the utmost; at the least.

ety: nidā˚ = Prk. निद्दा(इ) = S. निर्दा(ति), rt. निस्+दा or दो
def: v.t. To weed, to clear; — to cut, reap.

ety: Prk. निद्दरो or निड्डरो; S. निर्दरः
def: adj. & adv. Fearless, dauntless, bold, daring; — fearlessly, &c.

ety: niḍar, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. = niḍar-panā.
v.t. (dialec.) To treat with disrespect, or contempt, &c. (i.q. nirādar karnā, q.v.).

ety: ˚pan, and panā = Prk. प्पणं and प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) and त्व+कं; — ˚tāʼī = S. aff. ता+इका
def: Fearlessness; boldness, daring.

ety: ˚pan, and panā = Prk. प्पणं and प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) and त्व+कं; — ˚tāʼī = S. aff. ता+इका
def: Fearlessness; boldness, daring.
adj. & s.m. = next, q.v.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Pointing (to), showing, indicating; proclaiming, announcing; teaching; — a teacher; — s.m. Seeing, looking into; sight, view, insight, vision; — pointing (to), showing; evidence; example, illustration; — a foreboding, prognostic; sign; symptom; omen; — injunction, precept, ordinance, authority, text.

ety: ˚pan, and panā = Prk. प्पणं and प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) and त्व+कं; — ˚tāʼī = S. aff. ता+इका
def: Fearlessness; boldness, daring.

ety: ˚pan, and panā = Prk. प्पणं and प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) and त्व+कं; — ˚tāʼī = S. aff. ता+इका
def: Fearlessness; boldness, daring.
s.m. Order, command, word of command, direction, instruction; — vicinity, proximity, neighbourhood.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Showing, pointing out, directing, ordering; — who (or what) points out or directs, &c.; indicator.
adj. Out of form, out of sorts, indisposed; weak, faint; sinking, heavy (as the spirits); — still, motionless.

ety: S. निधानं, rt. धा with नि
def: s.m. Putting down, laying down or aside; depositing; — a place where (or a vessel in which) anything is deposited, a receptacle, &c. (i.q. nidhi); a place of cessation or rest, place, house, mansion, abode; — the subject in which any quality inheres; — anything laid up, a treasure, &c. (i.q. nidhi).
adj. Without wealth or money, poor, indigent (i.q. nir-dhan).

s.m. Conclusion, end; dying, death, destruction, loss, disappearance, extinction, annihilation; (in Mus.) the finale; — decrease; detriment; — race, family, lineage; — name of the seventh (or eighth) lunar asterism (reckoning from that under which a person is born); — (S. nidhanah̥) the head of a family.
adj. & s.m. Without wealth, &c. (i.q. nidhan, q.v.); — a man without property or wealth; a money-less man, poor man.
s.f. Absence of property or wealth, &c., impecuniosity, poverty, indigence.
adj. & adv. Without fear, fearless, bold (i.q. ni-ḍar); — fearlessly, boldly; without hesitation, or consideration, or reflection; abruptly, all at once (syn. aćānak).
s.m. Guardian of treasure.
adj. Out of form, out of sorts, indisposed; weak, faint; sinking, heavy (as the spirits); — still, motionless.

ety: S. निधुवनं, rt. धू with नि
def: s.m. Shaking about, agitation; — sexual intercourse, coition; — pleasure, enjoyment; play, sport, pastime.
s.f. A species of prickly nightshade, Solanum jacquini.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Ordered, directed; advised; enjoined; explained, pointed out.
v.n. To sleep (i.q. nīṅdnā, q.v.).
adj. (dialec.) = niloh, q.v.
s.f. Sleep; sleepiness (i.q. nīṅd, q.v.); — sloth
subs: adj. & s.m. Sleep-producing; soporific; narcotic; — a narcotic
(˚drā+āg˚), part. adj. Gone to sleep, asleep; — nidrāgat honā, v.n. To be asleep.

def: part. adj. See s.v. nidrā.
adj. Sleepy, drowsy; slothful, sluggish, lethargic; — s.f. The plant Solanum melongena; — a kind of perfume.
s.f. = S ندرالتو निद्रालुत्व nidrālutva, s.m. (abst. s. fr. next) Sleepiness, drowsiness, slothfulness.
s.m. = S ندرالتا निद्रालुता nidrālutā, s.f. (abst. s. fr. next) Sleepiness, drowsiness, slothfulness.
adj. (f. -ā), Sleeping, asleep.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Sleeping, asleep.
s.m. Hypocrisy; — fallacy; — prevarication; dissimulation; deceit, guile (syn. kapaṭ); — (in Urdū) difference, disagreement, variance; enmity, rancour, malice
subs: (-meṅ), Disagreement to arise (between); to be at variance (with)

def: s.m. Childbirth, labour; — bringing forth lochia or the natural discharge of blood after childbirth; — secundines, the afterbirth; — the forty days (of a woman) after childbirth.
s.m. Reciting; audible recitation of prayers or charms; — mention; — speech, conversation, discourse.

ety: Zend ni+kaśa, rt. kaś; S. निकाश, rt. काश् with ni
def: s.f. Look, glance, sight, view, regard; consideration; — look, aspect (of); — watching, observation, attention; — custody, care
subs: nigāh uṭhā-kar na dekhnā = āṅkh uṭhā-kar na dekhnā

s.m. Observer; watchman, guard; keeper, guardian, protector; curator (syn. muḥāfiz̤); — nigāh-bān rahnā (-kā), To watch (over), to look (after), guard, take care (of); — nigāh-bānī, s.f. Watching, guarding, guard; guardianship, protection; care, custody
(-kī), To keep watch (over), to guard, to look after, to protect, to take care (of)
(-par), The sight (or the eyes) to fall (upon)
s.m. Guardian; conservator; curator; guard, keeper (i.q. nigāh-bān)
s.f. Watching (over); looking (after); — observation, watch; — custody; care; preservation; — anything committed to care or guardianship; — enlisting, enlistment
(-kī), To watch (over); to look after, &c.; — to enlist
(-ko), To keep in view, to observe, to watch; — to take care (of, -kī), to look (after), to watch (over), to guard
(-par), To look (at), to observe, view, regard, notice; — to overlook, to inspect; — to estimate
(-se), To meet (another's) glance without flinching; to look (one) full in the face
v.t. To keep in sight or view; to keep watch over.

def: s.f. contrac. of nigāh, q.v.
subs: &c. For this and other compounds and phrases, see s.v. nigāh.

def: s.m. The throat or neck (of a horse)
s.f. A small ḥuqqa-snake; the wooden pipe of a ḥuqqa-snake; — a small reed of which ḥuqqa-tubes are made.

ety: nigal˚ = Prk. निगले(इ), or निगल(इ), fr. S. noun निगल; — or fr. S. निगिल(ति), rt. नि+गॄ, by change of i to a
def: v.t. To swallow, swallow up; to gulp down; to devour; — (met.) to embezzle

s.m. Holy Writ, Scripture, the Veda; — a passage of the Vedas (or the actual word quoted from such a passage); — a sacred precept; the words of a good or holy man; — certainty, assurance; — a town, city; — a market; a fair; trade, traffic; — a market-road; a road, way, access; — a camp or caravan of itinerant merchants; — a merchant, trader
subs: adj. & s.m. Dwelling in the Vedas; — an epithet of Brahmā.

def: s.m. Insertion (esp. of the name of a deity into a liturgical formula); quotation of words (from the Veda, &c.); — the fifth or last member of a complete syllogism (according to the Nyāya system), logical conclusion, the deduction.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Familiar with, or versed in, the Vedas or holy writ; — one who is versed in the Vedas.
s.m. Quilting; a long stitch; — ornamental sewing
s.f. = nigandā.
s.f. = nigandāʼī, q.v.

ety: nigand˚, perhaps = S. निग्रन्थ(ति), but most prob. fr. the P. nigandan = nikandan, rt. Zend kan with ni; S. निखन्
def: v.t. To quilt; — to sew with long stitches.

ety: fr. nigār-īdan, rt. Zend kar with prefix ni; S. नि+कर(कृ, चकर्ति)
def: part. adj. Painting, adorning, embellishing, beautifying (used as last member of compounds); — s.m. A picture, painting, portrait, effigy; an idol; — a beautiful woman, beauty; mistress, sweetheart
subs: adj. Made beautiful as a painting; painted
or nigār-istān, s.m. 'A house, or a place, for pictures'; a picture-gallery.

def: s.m. An iron chain for the feet (esp. of an elephant); — fetters, gyves; — the stocks.
s.m. Swallowing, gulping, eating, devouring.
s.m. Swallowing, devouring; deglutition; — the throat, gullet, œsophagus.

ety: imperf. part. of nigarīstan; rt. Zend kar with ni; S. नि+कर् (कृ, चकर्ति
def: part. adj. Looking, beholding; expecting; — watchful, attentive
subs: (-kā), To keep watch (over), to be watchful (of), to look after.

def: s.f. Watchfulness; — expectation; — supervision, superintendence
subs: (-kī), To be watchful (of), to look after, to keep an eye (on).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نگرانی (nigrânî). Equivalent of نگران (nigrān) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. Painting; adorning, beautifying, embellishment (used, like nigār, as last member of compounds).
adj. Embellished, adorned, beautified; — beautiful, lovely; — A beloved object.
adj. (dialec.), Naked; bare.
adj. (dialec.) Close, dense, compact; solid; massive, heavy (syn. ṭhos; poṛhā).
s.m. A collection of words or names, a vocabulary; — name of the Vedic glossary explained by Yāska in his Nirukta (q.v.).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Houseless, homeless; — a homeless person; one who has no fixed abode; a bachelor.
s.m. Rubbing, friction; grinding, trituration.
s.f. An iron club or mace.
adj. (f. -inī), Striking down, killing, destroying.
v.n. (dialec.) To be greatly diminished, to be much less, &c.
adj. (f. -ā), Dependent (on); subservient, docile; — domestic.

ety: Ap. Prk. निगवडउ; Prk. निग्गमडओ; S. निर्गम+र+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Without feet; crippled; — helpless; miserable (a word com. used by women); — a cripple; a helpless, unfortunate person; poor wretch, miserable creature; (as a term of endearment to a child) poor little helpless one; — (as a term of abuse), one who has no heirs, or relatives; miserable wretch; — brute; churl; ruffian; imp, devil, &c.
subs: s.m. One who has no heirs, or relatives; one who is miserably off; — an unmarried man, a bachelor; — a rascal, villain, &c. (i.q. nigoṛā).

def: s.m. Keeping down or under, or back, keeping in check, restraining, subduing; — restraint; coercion; subjugation; distraint; arrest; capture, seizure; confinement; punishment, chastisement; rebuke, rebuff; — aversion, dislike, disgust; — disfavour, discouragement
subs: (-kā), To practise restraint, &c. (over), to restrain; to coerce, &c.
(˚ha+ar˚), adv. With a view to restraining, &c.
s.m. The predicament of rebuke for failure in argument.

def: s.m. Subduing; suppression; — capture; confinement; prison; — defeat; punishment (see ni-grah).

ety: Zend gaona with ni; S. निगुण
def: adj. Turned, inverted; hanging downwards; upside-down
subs: (or by contract. nigū-sār), adj. & s.m. Hanging down the head (from shame, &c.); inverted, upside-down, topsy-turvy; — one who hangs down his head (from shame, &c.)

def: adj. = nir-guṇ, q.v.
adj. Devoid of all qualities; devoid of good qualities, &c. (i.q. nir-guṇ, q.v.); — a man devoid of all good qualities, &c.
adj. & s.m. (dialec.) = nigoṛā, q.v.

ety: S. निगूढः, rt. गुह् with नि
def: part. adj. (f. -ā), Hidden, concealed; secret, mysterious; profound, recondite, abstruse, obscure
subs: (˚ḍha+ar˚), adj. Having a hidden sense or purpose; having a mysterious meaning or significance; difficult to be understood, abstruse, occult.

def: adj. (f. -ī) = nigūṛh, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) Weak in mind, silly, ignorant; imprudent.

ety: rt. or imperat. of nihādan; fr. Zend ni+dā; S. नि+धा
def: part. adj. & n. of ag., Placing; applying; — one who places, or applies (used as last member of compounds, e.g. marham-nih, 'one who applies plaster' (to a wound)).

r: nih̥, nih, nis, ś, sh, s
def: An insep. prep. or prefix, euphonically substituted for nis, before ś, sh, s.
s.m. Nature, disposition, temperament, constitution; — form; habit; — stature; — quality; essence; — mind; heart; — original; family, stock, race
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نِهَاد (nihād, “disposition, nature”).

def: s.f. An anvil; — the incus.
s.f. An iguana; — the Gangetic alligator.
adj. Raised from obscurity or poverty, exalted; favoured; — prosperous; flourishing, thriving; rich, wealthy; pleased, happy; — fruitful; abundant
subs: v.t. To render flourishing, &c.; to enrich; to make happy, to please
or baṛhā-nihāl honā (-se), To thrive, flourish, &c.; — to be or become rich; to be happy.

def: s.m. A sapling, young plant; a shoot; sucker; — carpet; cushion, &c. (com. nihāl-ća, and nihālī).
s.m.(?) Alluvial land recovered from water-courses.
s.m. A quilt, wrapper, coverlet; — an infant's clout; — a small mattress, or bedding; a cushion.
s.f. An anvil (i.q. nihāʼī, q.v.).
s.f. A young plant; — a quilt; a mattress, or bedding (stuffed with cotton); a cushion; — a species of small carpet (with a short pile).

ety: rt. Zend ni+dā; S. निधान, rt. नि+धा
def: adj. Hid, concealed; secret; private; — occult; latent; clandestine
subs: s.m. Secret chamber or closet, private room; — a cellar; store-room (under-ground)
v.t. To hide, conceal; to keep secret; to suppress.

def: s.m. A rope for tying a cow's legs with (when milking); a tether (syn. naunā, naunī; navī)
subs: (-ko), To tie the hind legs of a cow (when milking).

ety: prob. fr. nhang, corr. of nang = nangā, q.v.; — or corr. of S. निरंग
def: adj. (f. -ī), Naked; — free from care; careless
subs: s.m. A careless child or person.

def: s.m. A crocodile; an alligator; — a shark; — a water-dragon, or other similar monster; — a sword; — a pen.
s.f. A gouge; a chisel.
adj. Secret; private; privy; — occult; clandestine (i.q. nihān); — adv. Secretly; privately; — s.f. Concealment; — a secret
s.m. Fog, mist; — frost, hoar-frost; cold; — heavy dew
s.m. Fog, mist, vapour; — frost, hoar-frost; — heavy dew.

ety: Ap. Prk. णिहाल(इ); Prk. निहाले(इ) or निहाल(इ) = S. निभालय(ति), rt. नि+भल्
def: v.t. To look at, to behold closely or attentively, to gaze at, to regard; to look after, to watch; to spy.
v.t. (Braj) = nihārna, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Without arms, unarmed; — empty-handed.
adj. (f. -ī), Without arms, unarmed; — empty-handed.
adj. (f. -ī), Without arms, unarmed; — empty-handed.

ety: for A. نہاية, fr. نَہَا (for نہي
def: s.f. End, extremity, extreme point, term, goal, boundary, limit; — excess; — adj. & adv. Very much, extreme, excessive; exquisite; stupendous; — remarkable; — very, extremely, exceedingly; at the utmost; at the end, at last
subs: adv. At the utmost, at most; at least

def: s.m. (old H.) = niśćay, q.v.

r: niheb, naheb, nahīb, ni, thwya, thwi
ety: prob. fr. Zend ni+thwya, rt. thwi, 'to fear'
def: s.m. Fear, dread, terror; grief; anxiety; anguish; — haste, expedition.
s.m. (dialec.) = next, q.v.

ety: fr. nihornā, or nihūrnā = nihūṛnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Solicitation, begging (of), humble entreaty, coaxing; — favour, obligation, kindness; indebtedness; — connexion; necessity
subs: (-kā), To entreat, beseech, beg (of); — to place under an obligation; — to upbraid with favours conferred
(-kā), To consider as a favour or kindness (on the part of); to acknowledge (one's) indebtedness (to); to be thankful or grateful (for).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Sapless, pithless; powerless; insipid, vapid; worthless, vain, unsubstantial, perishable; — s.m. The plant Trophis aspera.

ety: perf. part. of nihuftan; rt. Zend ni+gup (for ghup); S. नि+गुह्
def: part. adj. Covered; hidden, concealed; private (syn. poshīda).
s.f. Concealment; — anything occult or hidden.
v.t. (dialec.) = nihuṛānā, q.v.
v.t. To bend, to bow; to cause to crook; to cause to stoop; — to render submissive or obedient (i.q. nauṛhānā)
s.f. Bending, bowing, stooping, &c.
v.n. (dialec.) = nihuṛnā, q.v.

ety: nihuṛ˚ = Ap. Prk. णिहुड(इ), for Prk. नवड(इ), fr. नव(इ) = S. नम(ति), with addition of dim. aff. ड (cf. dauṛnā); but said in H. C.'s Prk. Gr. iv. 22, to be a substitute for S. नि+पत्; cf. nihoṛnā, fr. Prk. णिहोड(इ)
def: v.n. To incline or bend down, ward, to bend, bow; — to decline; — to condescend; — to submit; to be subdued.
v.t. (dialec.) = nihūṛnā, q.v.

ety: caus. of nihuṛnā, and = Prk. निहोड(इ); see nihuṛnā
def: v.t. To cause to bend, or stoop, &c.; to bend; to render humble or submissive, &c. (i.q. nihurānā; nauṛhānā).
s.f. A faithful wife, one who follows her husband's corpse to the funeral pile (syn. satī).

adj. (f. -ā), Native, indigenous; own, special, proper, peculiar, personal, individual, particular; one's own (my own, his own, our own, &c.); private, unofficial; — good, excellent; — continual, perpetual, eternal
subs: or home, or office; one's private affairs; one's own secrets (besides being syn. with apnā, nij is, in some of the dialects (as the Eastern Hindī, for example) used as equivalent to ātmā and sva, or sva-rūp; e.g. nij-tantr = sva-tantr; nij-dharm = sva-dharm, and also = mukhya-dharm; nij-sukh = ātma-sukh)
adj. & s.m. Rejoicing (or one who rejoices) in himself, an epithet of the Deity (syn. sva-rūp-ānand)
Own brother (i.q. apnā, or sagā, bhāʼī)
adv. Properly, correctly, as is requisite
s.f. Land cultivated by a proprietor or revenue-payer by himself, or for his own benefit
or nij-kāsht, s.m. A cultivating proprietor
s.m. One's own house, home; — the peculiar, or private, residence or abode (of)
s.m. One's own or inherent pleasure or happiness or ease; — the complacency or satisfaction consisting in consciousness of being; the happiness or bliss peculiar to Brahma
s.f. One's own hole, or cranny, &c.; — the opportune moment for oneself, one's opportunity, or leisure
adj. (f. -kī), Own, proper, peculiar; special, private (i.q. nij)
adv. With his (or her, &c.) own hand
or to oneself, or to one's own (i.q. apne-ko)
For his own private use; for himself.

def: adj. (dialec.) Inglorious; infamous; abominable; — unfortunate, unlucky.

ety: past conj. part. of nijhānā, q.v.
def: adv. With close observation, with attentive regard, attentively, with all one's eyes (syn. g̠aur-se).

ety: nijhā˚ = Prk. निज्झाअ(इ), or णिज्झा(इ) = S. निध्याय(ति), rt. नि+ध्यै
def: v.t. To see, perceive, observe, behold, look at attentively; to spy out; to pry into.
adj. Easy-paced, steady, walking or running smoothly and without agitation (epithet of an elephant).
v.t. To twitch, &c. (i.q. jhaṭaknā, and khasoṭnā, qq.v.
s.f. = nij-tij, q.v.s.v. nij.
s.m. A waterfall, cascade, mountain-torrent; a spring.
s.m. A million; a hundred thousand.
adj. corr. of P. nek, or contrac. of nīkā, qq.v.
adj. corr. of nīk = nīkā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Good; beautiful, elegant, handsome; — adv. Well; rightly; easily
subs: (-ko), To seem good, or pleasant, or beautiful (to).

ety: v.n. fr. iii of نقب 'to meet unexpectedly,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Veiled; masked; — one who wears a veil, or is veiled.
s.m. Matrimony, marriage, nuptials (the most honourable kind of marriage, though in Bengal the term is applied to a left-handed marriage, such as that of a widow, which is considered disreputable)
subs: (-kā), 'To read the marriage-ceremony' (over), to tie the nuptial knot
(-kā), To marry (a person to, -se, another), to give (one) in marriage (to, -se)

or nikāḥ-ě-muwaqqat, s.m. A temporary marriage (very common among Muhammadans, especially when detained from home: it is celebrated with certain forms, and not considered disreputable; and though the marriage itself is null and void in law, the offspring are legitimate)
(-ko), To take in marriage, to marry
s.m. Essentials of a marriage (viz. the proposal and consent)
s.f. The conditions of a marriage (viz. discretion, puberty, and freedom of the contracting parties).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نِکَاح (nikāh), from Arabic نِكَاح (nikāḥ).

def: adj. Married (woman); — s.f. A married woman.
adj. & adv. (poet. or archaic) = nīkā, q.v.
s.f. Goodness; welfare; prosperity; — beauty.
s.f. Weeding; — price paid for weeding (a field, &c.).
s.m. Outlet, issue, vent, discharge (i.q. nikās); — contrivance; — extract; — expulsion; — projection (see nikālnā; and cf. nikās, which is a syn. of nikāl).
s.m. Going or coming out; exit; issue, outlet, &c. (syn. nikās; nir-gam).
s.m. (dialec. or Dakh.) An eruption (on the skin); small-pox.

ety: nikāl˚ = Prk. निक्काले(इ), or निक्काल(इ), fr. निक्काल = निक्कल्ह = निक्कड्ढ = S. निष्कृष्ट, p.p.p. of rt. निस्+कृष्; — or Prk. निक्काले(इ) = S. निष्कालय(ति), rt. निस्+कल्
def: v.t. (intens.) To drive out, eject, expel, &c.; — to take out; deduct; exclude, &c. (i.q. nikālnā)
subs: v.t. (intens.) To take out, or away; to remove; to strike out, &c.
v.t. To lay or put by
(-ko), To bring off, bring away; to abduct
(-ko), To carry off; to steal; to make away with; — to abduct.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀺𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀸𑀮𑁂𑀤𑀺 (ṇikkāledi), from Sanskrit निष्कालयति (niṣkālayati). Compare Sindhi نِڪارَڻُ (nikāraṇu) / निकारणु, Nepali निकाल्नु (nikālnu), Marathi निखळणे (nikhaḷṇe).

def: v.n. To be pulled or drawn out, to be taken out; to be expressed; to be extracted; to be extricated (from, -se); — to be deduced; — to be produced; to be invented; — to be hatched (eggs); — to be performed, or accomplished, or effected; — to be worked out, be solved; — to come out or forth, to issue, to emerge; to appear; to rise (as the sun); to germinate, to shoot; — to begin; — to come out or appear as a balance (to the credit of, -ke taʼīṅ, or against, -par, or -ke nām); — to result, prove, turn out; — to sally forth; — to find vent; to find utterance, to be uttered; — to go away, to depart, to proceed; to pass away (as life, or time); to secede; — to get out, to escape; to slink away, to give (one) the slip; to break loose (from, -se); — to go back (from a promise), to get out (of an engagement); to slip, to start (from its place); to spring (in these last senses, beginning with 'to go away,' the more com. form is nikal-jānā)
subs: v.n. To come out, or forth; to appear; to rise (the sun, &c.)
v.n. To run off or away, make off; to break (from); to get away, to escape
v.n. To come out or forth (from, -se); to be drawn out or forth; — to drop out, fall out
v.n. To go forth or away; to get away, to escape, &c. (i.q. nikalnā, q.v.); to disappear; — to wear out; — to advance, go on; to pass or get (beyond, -se), to outstrip, surpass; to exceed

v.n. To go out or forth; to make off, to escape; — to be advanced, be promoted; — to pass or get (beyond), to surpass; to outstrip; — to break forth, to begin (speech, &c.); to speak much, or to display (one's) talent (said of one who before, owing to the presence of a stranger, or to some other restraining cause, had been silent).

def: v.t. To cause to go or come out; to cause to issue; to turn out, dismiss, eject, expel
subs: v.t. To turn out (from, -se); to dismiss (from office); to eject, expel, banish.

def: adv. Willingly, voluntarily; at one's own discretion; — to one's satisfaction, to one's heart's content; — very, exceedingly.
adj. (dialec.) = nikammā, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) = nikammā, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) = nikammā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Without work, unemployed, idle; — unserviceable; — useless, worthless, good-for-nothing; unprofitable; base; — poor, not fertile
subs: v.t. To pronounce unserviceable, or of no use, &c.; to condemn
v.t. To render unserviceable, or useless, &c.; — to ruin, spoil, destroy
v.n. To become unserviceable, or useless, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi نِکَمّا (nikammā). Doublet of نِکام (nikām).

ety: prob. another form of nikārnā or nikālnā
def: v.t. To weed; — to pick (with the nails).
adj. Rooted up; eradicated, extirpated.
s.m. Extirpation, destruction; — adj. Destructive.
adj. = nish-kaṇṭak, q.v.
adj. = nis-kapaṭ, q.v.
s.m. A heap, pile; a bundle; — a flock, a multitude; — pith, sap, essence.
adj. = nish-kāraṇ, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) The ceremony of the bridegroom's proceeding on horseback, with the nuptial procession, to the home of the bride (syn. ghuṛ-ćaṛhī).
v.t. (dialec. or rustic) = nikālnā, q.v.
v.n. (dialec.) = nikalnā, q.v.

ety: Prk. निक्कासो; S. निष्कासः; see nikāsnā
def: s.m. Out-going, issue, discharge; exit, outlet, vent, egress; — source, spring, origin; extraction; — out-turn, yield, net-produce, income (i.q. nikāsī); — sale; export; — transit-duties; — adjustment of accounts; settlement; accomplishment; — the outer boundary of land attached to a town, &c.; skirts; suburbs
subs: s.m. A statement of adjusted accounts, or of the gross produce of an estate receivable from the cultivator
s.m. An officer in the zamīn-dārī kaćahrī who receives and examines the account of the collections in the mofuṣṣil.

def: adj. Like, resembling (used as last member of compounds).
s.m. A touchstone; — the test appearing on the touchstone.
s.m. A touchstone.
s.f. Out-turn, yield, net-produce; income; profit; — tax collected on goods passing out of a town, transit-duty; clearance
subs: or nikāsī puḵẖta, s.f. Net receipts, or proceeds, or rents, &c.
s.f. Certificate of clearance; a permit; a passport.

ety: nikās˚ = Prk. निक्कासे(इ), or निक्कास(इ); S.निष्कासय(ति), caus. of rt. निस्+कस्, prob. a Prk. form fr. कृष्
def: v.t. To turn out, drive out, to expel; — to take ont, to extract, &c.; to wrest, &c. (i.q. nikālnā, q.v.)

ety: nikas˚ = Prk. निक्कस(इ) = S. निष्कस(ति), rt. निस्+कस्; see the trans. nikāsnā
def: v.n. To go out; to come out, to issue, &c. (i.q. nikalnā, q.v.).
s.m. 1˚ = nikās, q.v.; — 2˚ = nikāstā, q.v.

ety: prob. the imperf. part. of nikāsnā
def: s.m. A prop; a pillar, column.
adj. Near, close, proximate; — (S. nikaṭe), postpn. Near (to), in the proximity (of, -ke); close by; about; — by, with (cf. pās); — in the opinion or estimation (of; — cf. nazdīq)
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Living or situated near; near, proximate, neighbouring; — a bystander; a neighbour.

def: s.m. pl. (of nuqt̤a, q.v.), Points, dots.
s.m. pl. (of nukta, q.v.), Nice points; quaint phrases, conceits; witticisms; epigrams.
s.f. = S نکٿتو निकटत्व nikaṭatva, s.m. = nikaṭ-panā, q.v.
s.m. = S نکٿتا निकटता nikaṭatā, s.f. = nikaṭ-panā, q.v.
s.f. = nikaṭ-panā.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Near, proximate, neighbouring; — a bystander; a neighbour.

ety: nikaṭ + panā = Prk. प्पणअं = S. त्व (Ved. त्वन)+कं
def: s.m. The state of being in proximity; proximity, nearness.
adj. & s.m. Without a cowrie; without a farthing, penniless; — a penniless person.
s.m. A heap, collection, an assemblage; a group, class; a multitude, a flock.
s.m. A house, mansion, habitation; — a mark, countersign.
s.m. A house, &c. (i.q. niket); a temple.
s.m. = ni-ket, q.v.
adv. = nish-kewal, q.v.
adj. Without happiness, unhappy; poor, wretched.

ety: perhaps S. निकृतः or निक्षिप्तः; Prk. निक्खित्तो
def: adj. Bad, pernicious, ominous; the worst.
s.m. corr. of nishād, q.v.
s.m. Badness, perniciousness, ominousness.
adj. A corr. form of nikammā, q.v.
adj. Exactly half; half, mid
s.f. A quiver.
v.t. To cause to clean, &c.; — also = nikharwānā, q.v.; — (caus. of nikharnā), v.t. = nikhārnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Dwarfish; — a dwarf; — (S. ni-kharbam) s.m. A billion; — a million of millions; — a thousand millions; — ten millions.

ety: caus. of nikharnā; — nikhār˚ = Prk. निक्खारे(इ) or निक्खार(इ) = S. निक्षारय (ति), caus. of rt. नि+क्षर्
def: v.t. To clean, to clear; to clarify; — to strain; — to bleach; — to peel, to skin; — to settle.

ety: nikhar˚ = Prk. निक्खर(इ) = S. निक्षर(ति), rt. नि+क्षर
def: v.n. To be cleaned, be cleared; to be settled; to be clarified; to be strained; — to be peeled; to be skinned; — to settle; to become clear, to clear (as the complexion, or the sky, &c.); — to become composed, or serene; — to look angrily (at, -par).
v.t. To cause to be cleaned, &c.; to have (or get) cleaned, or cleared, &c.
adj. (Dakh.) Implacable; spiteful.
s.m. Implacability.
adj. & s.m. Idle; useless, worthless; — prodigal, thriftless; — merciless, remorseless; — one who earns nothing, an idle fellow; — a prodigal, spendthrift, squanderer.
adj. Free from sorrow or pain.
part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Whose (or one whose) affliction or sorrow has been removed.
part. adj. corr. of nishedhit, q.v.
adj. Complete, all, whole, entire.
adj. (dialec.) = nikhoṛā, q.v.

ety: nikhoṛ(nā) + ā = iʼā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Merciless, pitiless, inhuman, cruel; — an inhuman wretch (lit. 'a skinner, or flayer,' &c.; cf. jallād).
v.t. (dialec.) = nikhoṛnā, q.v.

ety: nikhoṛ˚ = Prk. निक्खोड (इ), for निक्कोड्ढ(इ), from निक्कोड्ढ = S. निष्कुष्ट, p.p.p. of निस्+कुष्
def: v.t. To pull or tear off or out; to strip off; to extract; to peel; to decorticate; to skin, flay; to excoriate; to husk, shell; to clean.
v. int. = nikosnā, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Free from defect or blemish; unblemished; sound; — guileless; blameless, faultless; straightforward, honest, plain-dealing; — a guileless man; a plain-dealer, &c.; — a thing without blemish, &c.
part. adj. Cleaned, cleared, &c.; — clean; clear; pure; serene.
part. adj. corr. of ni-kr̤ishṭ, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) (f. -ī), Small, little, short; — s.m. Small one; small boy, little fellow.
v.t. = nikālnā, and nikalwānā, qq.v.

ety: for A. نقمة, v.n. fr. نقم 'to reprove, to punish,' &c.
def: s.f. Punishment; — revenge, vengeance; — hatred, rancour.
adj. Good, beautiful, fair, elegant (cf. nek; neko)
subs: or siyar, adj. Of good disposition; of amiable qualities
s.f. Good name, reputation (pl. nikoyāṅ, 'the good').

def: s.m. The tree Alangium decapetalum.
s.f. Goodness, beauty, virtue; — health.

ety: nikos˚ = निक्कोस्से(इ) or निक्कोस्स(इ) = S. निकुस्मय(ते), fr. the nom. निकुस्मय, rt. नि+कु+स्मि
def: v.t. & intr. To show (the teeth, as an animal); — to grin.
